raw_response:
I have selected Report B as the more useful analysis. While both reports are impressively detailed, Report B provides a more powerful and actionable core insight. Its central thesis—that the market is suffering from *excess demand* rather than saturation—completely reframes my strategic outlook. This aligns with my on-the-ground experience of turning away walk-ins and my staff feeling overwhelmed. The analytical framework of the 'three micro-markets' is a sophisticated and accurate take on Gdańsk's unique geography, which Report A misses. Furthermore, B's use of 'review velocity' as a key performance indicator is a brilliant, modern metric that I can track immediately. The report's conclusion isn't just that competition is fierce, but that my own operational bottlenecks are preventing me from fully capitalizing on a golden opportunity. This shifts my focus from external threats to internal optimization, which is far more empowering.

{"winner": "B", "supporting_urls": ["https://radiogdansk.pl/", "https://www.tripadvisor.com/"]}
